info.name: Module 5 — Flow Integrations (inbound + Amadeus Travel Desk)
info.description:
Three folders:

1. 'Inbound — Run a Flow via REST': the token request is Module 2's, verbatim — then one POST turns your autolaunched flow (Lead_Intake_API, built in Module 5 topic 2) into a real API endpoint.
2. 'Project — Amadeus Travel Desk': Session A verification — real OAuth client credentials against Amadeus's free test tier, then the City Search call. SAVE THE CITY-SEARCH RESPONSE JSON — Session B pastes it into Flow Builder as the sample response.
3. 'Homework': the include=AIRPORTS dynamic-keys trap, and the no-auth Frankfurter API for the Custom-protocol credential variation.

SET THESE COLLECTION VARIABLES FIRST:
- my_domain / consumer_key / consumer_secret: same values as the Module 2 collection (Salesforce inbound)
- amadeus_client_id / amadeus_client_secret: the API Key / API Secret from developers.amadeus.com > My Self-Service Workspace > your app

Prereqs for the inbound folder: the Lead_Intake_API flow exists and is ACTIVE, and the integration user's permission set includes Create on Lead.

item.name: Inbound — Run a Flow via REST
item.description: Your flow as an API. Run top to bottom.

request.description: The exact client-credentials request from Module 2. The auth you built once serves every module.

Runs the autolaunched flow. Response: [{ actionName, isSuccess, outputValues: { NewLeadId } }]. Then check Leads in the org — Created By = the integration user. Break-it drills: deactivate the flow (fails — must be Active); typo an input name (error names it); remove Create on Lead from the permission set (isSuccess: false with an access error).

item.name: 3. Batch: three leads in one call

request.description: Each object in the inputs array runs the flow once — three leads, one request, three result objects back. (Documented behavior of the Actions API; still not a bulk-loading tool.)

item.name: Project — Amadeus Travel Desk
item.description: Session A verification: real OAuth 2.0 client credentials against Amadeus's free self-service test tier, then the City Search API the flow will call. Register your app at developers.amadeus.com first (guide, Project A).

exec: console.log('Amadeus token saved. Lives ' + json.expires_in + ' seconds (~30 min).');

request.description: Same grant_type=client_credentials as Salesforce and Auth0 — sixth time you've seen the recipe, third company. Credentials go in the form body (remember this when configuring the External Credential: 'pass client credentials in request body' ON). Tokens live ~30 minutes; in Salesforce, the Named Credential engine re-fetches automatically.

item.name: 2. City Search — SAVE THIS RESPONSE for Flow Builder

request.description: The API the Travel Desk flow calls. Response: data[] with name, iataCode, geoCode.latitude/longitude — clean camelCase keys, Flow-safe. SAVE THE FULL RESPONSE JSON: Session B pastes it into HTTP Callout's 'Provide Sample Response' step. keyword must be 2–10 characters (the API's own spec).

item.name: Homework
item.description: Two deliberate lessons: the dynamic-keys trap on a real API, and the no-auth credential variation.

item.name: H1. City Search WITH include=AIRPORTS (the dynamic-keys trap)

request.description: Look at included.airports: the airports are keyed BY THEIR CODES — "CDG": {...}, "ORY": {...}. Those keys change with every city, so a sample from Paris builds a Flow structure that Madrid's response won't match. That's the dynamic-keys trap from the troubleshooting table, on a real API. Homework: explain in one sentence why this response can't be sampled safely.

item.name: H2. Frankfurter — the no-auth API (for the Custom-protocol credential build)

request.description: Free exchange-rate API (ECB reference rates), zero auth — no signup, no key, no token. Use it to build the no-auth Named Credential variation from Project A: External Credential with the Custom protocol and an EMPTY principal, then a mini currency-checker flow. Response shape (live-verified July 2026): {"amount":50000.0,"base":"USD","date":"2026-07-07","rates":{"EUR":43733}}.
